responses.content:
have you ever wanted to learn more about racism or how people were treated back in the day? Freedom Walkers and Freedom's Daughters are stories about 2 high school girls who fought for equal rights because of their skin color. both stories were similar because they both fought for their rights and what they differently one of they was fighting for a new school one of them were fighting for their rights on school bus each author focuses on how the girls were treated because they were of different race and how people did not treat them like everyone else because of their race. so they fought for their rights because they were a different race 
The main idea of Claudette Colvins story was about how  she sat on a seat on a bus but the white people wanted to replace her with a white person "
The white boy told Marshall to get up from the seat beside him. Marshall refused. Then the bus driver ordered the black teenagers to move, but they continued to sit where they were. Up North, they were accustomed to riding integrated buses and trains. They didn’t see now why they should give up their seats."because of her race  but the law said she can be their and even the cops tried ejecting her off the bus even though she paid for the seat and according to law she could be at that seat because she paid for it these develop the main idea showing how people were so racist back then  The story freedom daughters is to inform us on how Barbara Johns had struggles and resolved them even with people in her way we can see this by reading the story and looking at paragraph. Barbara Johns was brave by taking action when adults would not. The main idea of Barbara's story is how you can fight and stand up for equal rights, like how she wanted a new school because the school leaders said they were going to make a new school just for the blacks but they never did so she decided to take action against it so she planned her strategy for 6 months then she went to the auditorium and stood at the podium and she shoo'd all the teachers away then she stated "we want a new school or none"and then all the students cheered. Barbara also knew that taking action unlike how other adults would, could result in her going broke. Barbara's story showed me that it is important to fight for your rights and brave.What'd I've learned from these two stories is that even if people doubt you you still need to stand up for your rights no matter what your race is what I've learned from these two stories is to be brave to be fearless stand up and fight. we can see this in both stories like how Barbara Johns stood up for a new school when the school leaders would not build one even with the risk of going broke. and with Claudette Colvins story were people wanted to kick her off of a bus that she paid for and had the rights to be on but people wanted to kick her out so a white person can get on then she got arrested because of her race she was charged with assault and battery  for resisting arrest she was arrested because she stood up for her rights is what happend.
responses.bucket_rationale: The paragraph contains several grammar and punctuation mistakes, such as missing capitalization at the beginning of sentences, incorrect verb forms, and run-on sentences. These errors go beyond mere style or phrasing suggestions.
